summary_one_line: A byte run of repeated indic_category glyph codes that drives the syllable state machine into the broken-cluster (case 4) action.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes that map to a dense run of indic glyph categories (consonant/halant/etc.) so the Ragel syllable machine walks transitions into the indic_broken_cluster lookahead action at case 4, taking the True side. No magic CMP literal gates this branch; what matters is glyph-category content density, evidenced by the repeated 0xcb/0x0c/0x0b/0xed byte runs in the winner seeds.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive havoc-mutated seeds carry long repeated runs (e.g. cb cb cb cb, ed ed ed ed, 0c 00 00 ... cb 0c) that feed many same-category glyphs through info[p].indic_category(); the per-role overlay shows the winner reaches case 4 and the broken-cluster sister arms (case 15/16/12) while accumulating 14 True hits on the blocker, with is_one_of/is_consonant called 182/104 times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds are short and dominated by table-tag ASCII (kern, ke, jdfm) injected by I2S CMP substitution toward OpenType tag comparisons; they never build the dense indic-category run, so the machine takes only the False side (L: T=0 F=169 at case 4) and the branch stays blocked across all 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is plain naive havoc/byte mutation (havoc-only): with no CMP literal guarding case 4, naive's unconstrained byte churn freely produces the long same-byte glyph-category runs the state machine needs. The loser cmplog's input-to-state CMP substitution stage wastes mutation budget steering bytes toward intercepted compare operands (table tags like 'kern'), diverting it away from the category-density region, so the comparison-solving stage is a net handicap here and naive wins on havoc alone.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive > cmplog with delta technique I2S; naive is the winner.
naive resolved the branch in all 10 trials while cmplog was blocked in all 10.
The blocker is a Ragel switch arm that emits the indic_broken_cluster syllable on the True side.
The transition is selected purely by info[p].indic_category() against the machine key spans, not by any magic literal compare.
Winner seeds carry long repeated byte runs (glyph-category density) such as cb cb cb cb and ed ed ed ed.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: bd bf bd 55 55 55 15 bd cb cb ed ed ed ed ed ed

evidence_trail.claim: The winner reaches case 4 and accumulates True hits on the blocker that the loser never reaches.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 559  T=14 F=188  T=0 F=169  case 4:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser cmplog seeds are short and contain OpenType table-tag ASCII like 'kern' produced by CMP-driven byte insertion.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other intercepted operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive's only mutators are havoc + token byte mutators with no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: The winner is naive, not cmplog/vpc, so the I2S-floor (dash-row) lineage check does not apply: naive has no I2SRandReplace and emits no ParentInfo-only dash rows. The winner-resolving seeds are all fuzzer=naive (per BRANCH SEEDS), confirmed by mutation_op chains that are pure havoc operators (BytesSwapMutator, WordAddMutator, ByteFlipMutator, etc.). No mandatory I2S lineage verification is required because claimed_mechanism is havoc-only; cmplog is the loser here, so I2S is implicated as a handicap, not as the winning technique.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the case-4 transition is gated by an exact multi-byte literal compare (so I2S substitution is required to satisfy it) and cmplog then beats naive on reaching case 4 — that would show this branch is an I2S magic gate rather than a category-density region naive reaches by free byte churn.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that cmplog's table-tag ASCII (kern/ke) is actively diverting mutation budget away from the category-density region is inferred from seed content; the byte diff shows differences but does not prove I2S caused cmplog's failure rather than mere trial variance.
